ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for popiah skin; mapped to wheat flour food product as the closest defensible parent class. Popiah skin is a paper-thin cooked wheat flour and water wrapper used in Singaporean and Chinese cuisine.
ontology.foodex2_description: Wheat flour-based flatbread / wrapper — FoodEx2 composite grain product category used as closest defensible anchor for thin wheat flour wrappers.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code for popiah skin exists; A00LC (wheat-based flatbread/grain product) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for popiah skin or equivalent thin wheat flour wrapper product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard popiah skin sheet weighs approximately 25–35 g and is used as a single wrapper for one popiah roll.

nutrient_source.title: Popiah Skin — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Popiah Skin (F05030015). All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this primary regional source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — wheat flour-based flatbread/wrapper analogous estimate; closest approved GI reference: white wheat flour tortilla/flatbread GI ~52 (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for popiah skin found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on thin wheat flour flatbread/tortilla entries (GI ~52, low category) from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (52 × 13.3) / 100 ≈ 6.9 for a 30 g serving (13.3 g available carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content at 80 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Popiah skin alone is low in sodium (80 mg/100 g) and is suitable for individuals managing hypertension; however, total dish sodium rises substantially with fillings, sauces, and condiments, so the complete popiah assembly should be assessed rather than the skin in isolation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (45.7 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (2.2 g/100 g) and a GI of approximately 52
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit popiah skin portions to 1–2 sheets per meal, pair with high-fibre and protein-rich fillings to blunt postprandial glucose response, and consider wholemeal or high-fibre wrapper alternatives where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat (1.0 g/100 g), negligible saturated fat (~0.2 g/100 g), and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Popiah skin has a favourable lipid profile with very low total fat and negligible saturated fat, making it a low cardiovascular concern as a wrapper; overall dish cardiovascular impact depends on the fat and sodium content of fillings and sauces used.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Popiah skin (薄饼皮, Kulit Popiah) is a paper-thin, round wheat flour wrapper used in Singaporean and Chinese cuisine as the outer casing for popiah, a fresh spring roll popular in Singapore, Malaysia, and the Fujian Chinese diaspora. It is made from a simple batter of wheat flour and water, spread thinly onto a hot flat pan and cooked briefly until just set, producing a soft, pliable, slightly translucent crepe-like sheet. The texture is smooth and delicate, with a neutral, mildly floury taste that serves as a neutral vehicle for savoury fillings such as braised turnip, bean sprouts, tofu, egg, and prawns. Nutritionally, popiah skin is a moderate-energy, low-fat, high-carbohydrate food providing approximately 227 kcal, 8.4 g protein, 1.0 g fat, and 45.7 g carbohydrate per 100 g, with 2.2 g dietary fibre and 80 mg sodium. The low fat and sodium content make the skin itself suitable for hypertension and cardiovascular health, but the refined carbohydrate content and low fibre-to-carb ratio require portion awareness for individuals with type 2 diabetes. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low category) based on analogous thin wheat flour flatbread data. Popiah skin is widely available fresh or frozen in Singapore supermarkets and wet markets and is a staple component of the traditional popiah dish served at breakfast, lunch, or as a snack.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F05030015), which is the primary approved regional source for Singapore foods. All macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches. GI is an analogous estimate from an approved peer-reviewed source (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) as no direct GI measurement for popiah skin exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for popiah skin are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F05030015 as of April 2026. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.2 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central entry for wheat flour tortilla (FDC ID 1100612) as the closest approved analogous product; HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this item. sugar_g estimated at 0.5 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central wheat flour tortilla data; HPB does not report sugar separately. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source — this is a plain wheat flour and water product containing no animal-derived ingredients. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on thin wheat flour flatbread/tortilla data from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283; no direct GI measurement for popiah skin was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated for a 30 g serving: (52 × 13.71) / 100 ≈ 6.9. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest defensible anchor for wheat-based flatbread/wrapper; no dedicated code exists for popiah skin.
